datasets.name: ds_kpi_return
queryLines:
SELECT CAST(date_trunc('week', date) AS DATE) AS week,
  max_by(cum_port_ret * 100, date) AS return_pct
FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.portfolio_performance WHERE portfolio_id = 1
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

datasets.name: ds_kpi_outperf
queryLines:
SELECT CAST(date_trunc('week', date) AS DATE) AS week,
  max_by((cum_port_ret - cum_bench_ret) * 100, date) AS outperf_pts
FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.portfolio_performance WHERE portfolio_id = 1
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

datasets.name: ds_kpi_conc
queryLines:
SELECT CAST(date_trunc('week', date) AS DATE) AS week,
  max_by(core_ai_pct / 100.0, date) AS core_ai
FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.concentration_timeseries WHERE portfolio_id = 1
GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

datasets.name: ds_kpi_holdings
queryLines: SELECT count(*) AS holdings FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.holdings_enriched WHERE portfolio_id = 1

datasets.name: ds_var95
queryLines: SELECT var_dollar, var_pct FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.var_metrics WHERE regime='Post-pivot (current risk)' AND confidence=0.95

datasets.name: ds_var99
queryLines: SELECT var_dollar, var_pct FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.var_metrics WHERE regime='Post-pivot (current risk)' AND confidence=0.99

datasets.name: ds_perf
queryLines:
SELECT date,
  (1 + cum_port_ret) * 100 AS portfolio,
  (1 + cum_bench_ret) * 100 AS benchmark
FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.portfolio_performance WHERE portfolio_id = 1 ORDER BY date

datasets.name: ds_sharpe_scatter
queryLines:
SELECT ticker, company, sector, ai_exposure,
  annualized_return, annualized_risk, sharpe_ratio,
  coalesce(fund_weight, 0) AS fund_weight
FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.sharpe_analysis WHERE fund_weight IS NOT NULL ORDER BY sharpe_ratio DESC

datasets.name: ds_conc
queryLines:
SELECT CAST(date_trunc('week', date) AS DATE) AS week,
  round(avg(core_ai_pct), 1) AS core_ai_pct,
  round(avg(core_ai_pct + ai_adjacent_pct), 1) AS ai_exposed_pct
FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.concentration_timeseries WHERE portfolio_id = 1 GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY week

datasets.name: ds_rebal
queryLines:
SELECT r.rebalance_date, r.ticker, s.company, r.action,
  r.old_weight, r.new_weight, r.weight_change, r.rationale
FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.rebalances r JOIN main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.securities s ON s.ticker = r.ticker
ORDER BY r.rebalance_date DESC, abs(r.weight_change) DESC

datasets.name: ds_risk
queryLines:
SELECT period, sort_order, fund_sharpe, fund_volatility, fund_return,
  bench_sharpe, bench_volatility, bench_return
FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.risk_metrics ORDER BY sort_order

datasets.name: ds_alloc
datasets.displayName: Allocation: fund vs benchmark by sector
queryLines: SELECT sector, fund_weight * 100 AS portfolio_weight, benchmark_weight * 100 AS benchmark_weight

datasets.name: ds_dist
queryLines:
SELECT return_bucket_pct, days,
  CASE WHEN return_bucket_pct < 0 THEN 'Down day' ELSE 'Up day' END AS direction
FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.returns_distribution ORDER BY return_bucket_pct

datasets.name: ds_news
queryLines:
SELECT n.published_date, n.source, n.title, n.sentiment_label, n.sentiment, ne.ticker
FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.news_enriched ne JOIN main.dbdemos_aibi_fsi_portfolio_assistant.news n ON n.article_id = ne.article_id
WHERE ne.ai_exposure = 'Core AI' ORDER BY n.published_date DESC LIMIT 50

lines:
# AI Growth Fund — winning, but how exposed?

Our flagship fund is beating its benchmark by riding the AI rally. The catch: a series of reorgs piled it into AI names — and one major pivot took core-AI exposure from ~30% to ~80%. Great returns, but how much risk are we carrying if the AI trade turns?

frame.title: Cumulative return — AI Growth Fund vs benchmark (indexed to 100)

lines:
## How much could a bad day cost?

**Value at Risk** puts a dollar figure on the downside (on a **$100M** book): on 95% of days the fund won't lose more than the figure below — but concentrating into AI pushed that number up. The chart shows which holdings actually reward their risk: **up** = more return, **right** = more risk, **size** = position weight. Best is top-left; the fund's AI names (coral) sit to the right.

frame.title: Risk vs return by holding — each bubble is a stock (hover for details)

lines:
# Why the strong returns come with risk

The outperformance is concentrated in AI. A series of reorgs — capped by one major pivot — sold the fund's defensives and bought AI, taking core-AI exposure from ~30% to ~80%. That single decision drove the returns and the risk at the same time.

## 1. The reorgs that piled into AI

Three portfolio reorganizations, ending with a major pivot on **Aug 4, 2025** that sold every defensive (JNJ, PG, KO, XOM, JPM) and concentrated the book in core-AI names.

frame.title: Portfolio reorganizations — what was sold and bought

lines:
## 2. Concentration stepped up at the reorg

Core-AI exposure was steady near 30%, drifted up with two tilts, then **jumped to ~80% at the August pivot** — and has stayed there. The risk is now structural, not incidental.

frame.title: Core-AI concentration over time — jumps from ~30% to ~80% at the August pivot

lines:
## 3. Is the risk paying off — so far? (Sharpe)

The bet has worked *to date*: after the major pivot the fund's volatility **rose** (from ~23% to ~29%), but returns rose even faster, so its Sharpe is strong (~2.1, above the benchmark's ~1.76). The caution: that reward rides on a single, undiversified ~77% core-AI position — the higher volatility and Value-at-Risk are the price of it, and they bite hard if the AI trade turns.

frame.title: Risk-adjusted return — before vs after the major pivot
Daily return distribution (post-pivot) — the downside tail

lines:
## 4. The fund is far more exposed to Technology than the benchmark

~87% Technology vs ~34% in the benchmark, and over half the book in core-AI names — a turn in the AI trade would hit it disproportionately.

frame.title: Sector allocation — fund vs benchmark (Technology is the overweight)
Weight by AI exposure — 80% core AI

lines:
## 5. The news flow on those AI names

Recent sentiment on the fund's core-AI holdings — the qualitative signal a manager watches for an early turn.
